backupScope: database schema/data/indexes/roles/permissions/Auth users/migration history/encryption root key; excludes custom role passwords and non-database project configuration
restoreSource: hash-bound Supabase backup metadata record selected after watermark verification
restorePoint: first completed physical backup whose provider inserted_at is at or after the post-watermark source-integrity snapshot; watermark and normalized-data containment are proven only after restore
rtoStartEvent: UTC timestamp at the owner-approved PR12-ACTION-017 Restore to a New Project action start, before the provider restore operation begins
rtoEndEvent: UTC timestamp after target availability plus migration, schema/data, Auth, tenant, Data API, GraphQL, service-role, and external-side-effect gates pass; evidence privacy is a later release-evidence gate and is not part of service RTO
rtoMeasurementClockAndSource: operator monotonic elapsed interval from PR12-ACTION-017 action start through PR12-CMD-019F completion, corroborated by Supabase provider, source database, restore database, and operator UTC
rpoWatermarkDefinition: The committed updated_at value of the fixed synthetic reservation included in the selected physical backup
rpoObservationEvent: UTC timestamp immediately before restore confirmation
rpoMeasurementClockAndSource: source database clock_timestamp() UTC minus the restored watermark; operator UTC is an independent skew-bound anchor and the restore database proves the restored watermark

method.restore: Supabase Dashboard Restore to a New Project (Beta)

includedScope: database schema, tables, views, procedures, and indexes
Auth user data and password hashes
encryption root key; Vault secrets and encrypted columns may remain readable on the restore target, but their values must never enter evidence

excludedOrManualScope.authSettingsAndApiKeys: RECONFIGURE_WITH_TARGET-SPECIFIC KEYS; NEVER COPY VALUES INTO EVIDENCE
excludedOrManualScope.customDatabaseRolePasswords: NOT_STORED_IN_DAILY_BACKUPS; prove no custom password is required or reset through an owner-approved secret channel and revalidate before PASS
excludedOrManualScope.extensionsAndSettings: enabled extension catalog state may be copied with the database; extension and platform settings require explicit post-restore reconfiguration/verification
excludedOrManualScope.readReplicas: NOT_COPIED; separately reconfigure only under owner approval
excludedOrManualScope.vaultSecrets: ENCRYPTION_ROOT_KEY_IS_TRANSFERRED; never retrieve or capture values, and prove external-operation paths are disabled before and after restore

watermark.writeMethod: single owner-approved synthetic update committed before the eligible backup
watermark.candidateSql: update public.reservations set updated_at = clock_timestamp() where id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-00000000f005';

rto.startEvent: UTC timestamp recorded when the owner-approved PR12-ACTION-017 Restore to a New Project action starts, before the provider restore operation begins
rto.endEvent: UTC timestamp after target availability plus migration, schema/data, Auth, tenant, Data API, GraphQL, service-role, and external-side-effect gates pass; evidence privacy is outside service RTO
rto.clockSource: operator monotonic elapsed interval from PR12-ACTION-017 through PR12-CMD-019F, corroborated by provider/source-database/restore-database/operator UTC

rpo.durableWatermarkDefinition: The committed updated_at value of the fixed synthetic reservation included in the selected physical backup
rpo.observationEvent: UTC timestamp immediately before restore confirmation
rpo.clockSource: source database clock_timestamp() UTC is the calculation clock; operator UTC independently corroborates skew and restore database UTC proves the restored watermark
rpo.limitation: This static synthetic drill measures recovery-point age; it does not model continuous production writes.

postRestoreRequired: normalized schema and data hash parity
all explicit and derived row counts
fresh Auth sign-in and refresh with target-issued tokens
Data API ACL and RLS matrix
abortOn: no eligible physical backup within 36 hours
restore integrity, Auth, tenant, API, or privacy failure
owner-approved maximum clock skew is missing or exceeded
manual workaround, dump editing, or destructive in-place restore requirement

backupEvidence.requiredRecord: hash the normalized backup metadata record derived from a fresh Management API inventory and containing command/runtime identity, backup ID/type/status/provider inserted_at/observation UTC, source ref fingerprint, temporal eligibility, the PR12-CMD-017 operation binding, post-watermark source-integrity binding, and the raw provider-response binding; watermark and normalized-data containment remain PROVEN_ONLY_AFTER_RESTORE

operationEvidence.restoreCreationRequiredFields: command ID PR12-ACTION-017, provider operation identifier availability with null when not exposed, source ref, selected backup ID/hash, source watermark, Dashboard action evidence, created project ref/name/region/compute/database version, provider created_at, restore-ready observation UTC, mirrored disk/SSL/network comparison, quote evidence, pre-action source database UTC, operator UTC wall-clock anchor, operator monotonic start, source-database and operator RPO observation UTC before confirmation; restore database UTC and system_identifier are deliberately excluded before supplemental connection approval
operationEvidence.postRestoreRequiredFields: the first supplemental-approved read-only identity/clock command captures restore database clock_timestamp() UTC; PR12-CMD-019F records source/restore refs, restored watermark, PASS, operator monotonic end and elapsed interval, and completion UTC at command end

productTargetConflict.releaseMeaning: Passing this 8h/24h drill does not prove the stricter 30m/15m product target; commercial release remains NO_GO until a named owner hash-binds a formal release-authority decision or the stricter target is measured and passed.
